description: Project-planner agent step schemas. The plan step reads a project README goal, surveys the issue landscape, and emits proposed_issues[] (deferred_items-compatible) to close the gap between goal and current state.

plan.description: Closure step output for the plan phase. proposed_issues items are structurally identical to considerer deferred_items so the orchestrator issue-emitter can consume them without transformation.

action.description: closing = emit proposed issues and finish. repeat = re-examine (e.g. README unclear).

goal_statement.description: The goal text extracted verbatim (or near-verbatim) from the project README.

extraction_method.description: How the goal was located in the README. readme_heading = first H1/H2 sentence, readme_body = full body paragraph, readme_list = enumerated goal items.

existing_issue_count.description: Number of open issues already in the project at plan time.

gap_summary.description: Brief description of what the goal requires that existing issues do not yet cover.

coverage_axes.description: Axes (dimensions) of the goal that proposed_issues collectively cover. Each axis maps to one or more proposed_issues by index.

axis.description: Short name of the coverage dimension (e.g. 'schema', 'CLI', 'docs', 'tests').

description.description: What this axis covers in the context of the project goal.

issue_indices.description: Zero-based indices into proposed_issues[] that address this axis.

proposed_issues.description: Issue candidates to close the gap between the project goal and the current issue landscape. Structurally compatible with considerer deferred_items — the orchestrator issue-emitter consumes these via the same outbox path.

title.description: Issue title. Self-contained, no reference to the parent issue number required (the runner links it back via body).

body.description: Issue body in markdown. Should restate the scope concretely so the downstream agent does not need to re-read the parent issue.

labels.description: Labels applied to the new issue. Usually 'kind:impl' or 'kind:consider'. Omit 'order:N' — the triager assigns it.

projects.description: Project bindings for the new issue. Absent = inherit parent's projects (when inheritProjectsForCreateIssue is on). Empty array = explicit opt-out (no inheritance). Non-empty array = use this explicit list, parent ignored.

verdict.description: Always 'done' for the plan step — the planner emits issues and finishes.

final_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what was planned and why.
